## [00:00 - 02:41]

Now, what if you need to hire both setters and closers? Which ones do you hire first? Well, that's exactly what we're going to cover in this training. We're going to cover should you hire setters first or closers first. And we're also going to cover what to do when you're kind of caught in the middle. So again, to review, hire based on constraints. So you should look at your constraints and just hire based on what's going to alleviate your constraints. So if your constraint is opportunity volume and or lead efficiency, you need to hire setters. Like if you need more appointments, you need to hire setters. But if your constraint is calendar availability, then you need to hire closers. Okay. And that could be because you're taking all the calls or you don't have enough closers. Then you need to hire closers. Now, there is a specific scenario and this comes up more nowadays with the group funnels where you kind of get caught in the middle. So sometimes you'll be doing all the setting and all the closing. Okay. And so it's like, well, dude, what do I hire first? Because I'm doing both. Well, in this case, I would hire a setter first. Why would I hire a setter first? Because a setting is a lower leverage activity than closing. There you go. Right. And we talked about that in the when the hire closers training high and low leverage activities. So just make sure you go through the requirements in the previous training on when the hire setters are sorry. Yeah, when the hire setters, I'd hire the setter, get them trained, and then you essentially become a full time closer. But what's going to happen is once the setters dialed in or you get two setters and they're dialed in, your counter is going to be overflowing because you probably weren't maxing out the opportunity in your group or your dials or whatever. Because you were kind of like juggling a few things. So you're going to do the hire a closer probably four weeks after you hire like two one or two setters. Okay. So just know that closer is going to become in second. Now, there is an exception. If you do come across like a stud closer who like, you know, this person is just going to crush, then you can hire them first and then essentially you go full time setter until you can quickly hire a setter. Because that's going to be the next step. Okay. So that's actually what I did is I, you know, when, when we were, you know, a while ago we were scaling up our B2B offer. Me and Mitchell started working together and he's a stud closer and we've worked together for a long time. So, you know, I just, I'm not going to make him be a setter like he's already good enough to close. So I did all of the setting and then very, very quickly we brought in the center. So that was an exception. It's a very rare opportunity in general. The best practice is to go setter than to go closer. Okay. So, um, it's also a good opportunity if you feel like your curated opportunity is low opportunity volume and you really need to take some time to kind of go full time setter.
